pattern: Hashing one list's positions lets a single pass over the other find every shared entry while tracking the smallest combined index, collecting all ties.
hints: You need strings present in both lists, ranked by the sum of their positions.
Store each string of the first list with its index in a hash map for O(1) lookups.
Scan the second list; for any string found in the map compute the index sum, keep the running minimum, and gather every string that reaches that minimum.

edge_inputs.description: statement example two least index sum
statement example three with a tie
multiple ties at index sum zero impossible but minimal at front

stress.description: two shuffled lists guaranteed to share entries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(80, size))
    base = ['item' + str(i) for i in range(n)]
    list1 = base[:]
    rng.shuffle(list1)
    list2 = base[:]
    rng.shuffle(list2)
    cut1 = rng.randint(n // 2, n)
    cut2 = rng.randint(n // 2, n)
    list1 = list1[:cut1]
    list2 = list2[:cut2]
    s1 = set(list1)
    if not (s1 & set(list2)):
        list2[0] = list1[0]
    return [list1, list2]
